report_name: Mandiant Report: AI-Driven Disinformation Poses Greatest Threat to 2028 U.S. Elections

threat_actors.name: AI-Powered Disinformation Actors
threat_actors.description: A general class of actors, including nation-states, domestic extremist groups, and individuals, who leverage AI for influence operations to sow discord and influence voters, as described in the Mandiant report on threats to the 2028 U.S. elections.
threat_actors.goals: To manipulate public opinion, suppress voter turnout, and erode trust in the democratic process using AI-generated disinformation.

attack_patterns.name: Social Media
attack_patterns.description: Actors scrape social media and public records to build profiles of target demographics for influence operations.

attack_patterns.name: Social Media Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Actors establish thousands of fake social media accounts to be used by AI agents for distributing disinformation.

attack_patterns.name: Generative AI
attack_patterns.description: Actors use open-source AI models, such as large language models and diffusion models, to generate hyper-realistic disinformation content.

attack_patterns.name: Defacement
attack_patterns.description: The report characterizes the cognitive impact of sowing discord and undermining trust in institutions as a form of defacement of public discourse.

attack_patterns.name: Generate Deepfakes
attack_patterns.description: Actors use AI to generate convincing fake videos, audio clips, and text of political candidates, officials, and news events to manipulate public opinion.

attack_patterns.name: Deploy AI Agent
attack_patterns.description: Actors deploy AI-powered bots or agents that can engage in real-time, context-aware conversations on social media to push a narrative, a technique referred to as 'AI-ad-libbing'.

tools.name: Generative AI
tools.description: Open-source AI models, including LLMs and diffusion models, are used to create hyper-realistic deepfake content for disinformation campaigns.

identities.name: United States Electorate
identities.description: The voting public of the United States, identified as the primary target for manipulation, voter suppression, and erosion of trust through AI-driven disinformation campaigns.

identities.name: United States Government
identities.description: The integrity of the U.S. democratic process and institutions are targeted by AI-powered disinformation campaigns.

infrastructure.name: Disinformation-as-a-Service (DaaS) Platform
infrastructure.description: Emerging dark web platforms that automate the creation and distribution of synthetic content for influence operations, making sophisticated campaigns accessible to a wide range of actors.

infrastructure.name: Fake Social Media Account Network
infrastructure.description: A network of thousands of fake social media accounts created to be operated by AI agents for the purpose of spreading disinformation and manipulating online discourse.
